title: Ficus superba
taxonomy: Ficus superba is classified within the family Moraceae, commonly known as the mulberry or fig family. This classification places it among the approximately 50 genera within the family Moraceae. The genus Ficus, to which Ficus superba belongs, comprises around 850 to 1000 species, many of which are native to tropical regions. The species Ficus superba itself is considered to be a tree or shrub native to Australia. Through its classification, Ficus superba is recognized as a member of the larger plant kingdom and is studied alongside other species within the Moraceae family.
